title: quercus faginea var. submembranacea
intro: Quercus faginea var. submembranacea, also known as the Portuguese oak or evergreen oak, is a variety of the Quercus faginea tree native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high tannin content. The health benefits of Quercus faginea var. submembranacea include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to skin infections.
